prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions (f⁺ electrophilicity, f⁻ nucleophilicity, f⁰ radical susceptibility, and the dual descriptor) for aniline; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic index f⁺.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI (density-fitted Coulomb integrals) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz; single-point charges evaluated at this fixed geometry (no re-optimization) for the N, N+1, and N–1 states.
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1) for the parent, doublets for the ±1 charge states as required by the method.
Software/backend: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF backend; version as bundled in chemkit).
Convergence & caveats: SCFs for the neutral, cation, and anion all converged; integrity.status = "ok" and integrity.trustworthy = true. Engine returned three warnings (verbatim below) noting charge-conservation checks and header presence. No other warnings or fallbacks were reported.

Selected numerical results (Mulliken population analysis, charges in e)
• Most electrophilic site (max f⁺): atom 3 (C, index 2)  f⁺ = 2.2975867 e
• Most nucleophilic site (max f⁻): atom 1 (N, index 0)  f⁻ = 0.20452359 e
• Sum of f⁺ = 1.0000 e; sum of f⁻ = 1.0000 e (within conservation tolerance)
Full per-atom f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, and dual-descriptor arrays are available in the result JSON at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpqdzgbemj/live.json.

Live log & files
The run was executed via:
/…/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpqdzgbemj/live.json
A live engine transcript was streamed during execution to the default fukui_<timestamp>.out file in the caller’s working directory; you can tail that file for the full SCF and Mulliken-population details. The per-atom plot is saved at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/argo_o3/20260703-185716_aniline_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.

Tool warnings (verbatim)
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

Headline result (largest electrophilic Fukui index): 2.2975867 e
